Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The 2014 Berlin Marathon was the 41st edition of the Berlin Marathon. The marathon took place in Berlin, Germany, on 28 September 2014 and was the fourth World Marathon Majors race of the year. The men's race was won by Kenyan athlete Dennis Kimetto in a world record time of 2:02:57, breaking the previous record of Wilson Kipsang by 26 seconds. Kimetto's performance means that the last six men's world records at the distance have all been set at the Berlin Marathon. Runner-up Emmanuel Mutai also surpassed Kipsang's old mark, finishing in 2:03:13. B. The 41st installment of the Berlin Marathon event occurred in 2014. This marathon competition was held in Berlin, Germany, on September 28, 2014, and represented the fourth World Marathon Majors event of that particular year. In the men's competition, the victory was secured by the Kenyan athlete Dennis Kimetto, who established a new world record with a time of 2 hours, 2 minutes, and 57 seconds. This impressive feat surpassed the prior record held by Wilson Kipsang by a margin of 26 seconds. Kimetto's exceptional showing signifies that every one of the most recent six world records for men in this distance discipline has been established during the Berlin Marathon. The athlete who finished in second place, Emmanuel Mutai, also managed to exceed Kipsang's former record, completing the race in 2 hours, 3 minutes, and 13 seconds. During his progression to his runner-up position, Mutai additionally achieved a world record for the 30-kilometer distance, recording a time of 1 hour, 27 minutes, and 37 seconds. The women's race saw Tirfi Tsegaye from Ethiopia claim the win, achieving a finish time of 2 hours, 20 minutes, and 18 seconds. Securing the third position in a
C. The Berlin Marathon was held on 28 September 2014, and it was the 41st edition of the event. Dennis Kimetto, a Kenyan Olympian, won the men's race in a world record time of 2:02:57, breaking Wilson Kipsang's previous record by 26 seconds.
